intro: Antherotoma fenarolii, also known as the Antherotoma tree, is a plant species native to tropical Africa. The Antherotoma tree has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its numerous health benefits, including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Antherotoma fenarolii is often prepared as a tea or tincture to harness its health benefits, and is also used in some commercial herbal remedies, including cough syrups and anti-inflammatory creams.
